findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 44. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: Repeated header/footer content inserted between page-equivalent sections in the HTML disrupts reading order
findings.evidence: In the HTML, the full memorandum header block (State of NC address, officials' names, MEMORANDUM heading, To/From/Subject, signature) is repeated — appearing once as page 1 content and then again at the top of the next HTML chunk before the body text continues. In the PDF, this header appears only once on page 1.

findings.description: The HTML adds internal anchor links in the table of contents (href="#history-background", etc.) that do not exist as hyperlinks in the source PDF. These are navigation anchors linking to section IDs within the document.
findings.evidence: PDF TOC is plain text: 'I. History/Background' etc. HTML wraps these in <a href="#history-background"> etc. However, these are internal navigation enhancements, not fabricated external URLs.
